Test qos strategy -- test netprofile basic behaviour
Utility)
testbasec
TestQosFunctionc
superr
myinitZ
_start_ovsZ
_start_netagent)
self
	__class__
A/usr/lib/python3.9/site-packages/na_tests/ft/test_qos_function.pyr
TestQosFunction.myinitc
cd {test_dir}
ovs-vsctl add-br br0 -- set bridge br0 fail-mode=secure --     add-port br0 p1 -- set interface p1 ofport_request=1 type=dummy --     add-port br0 p2 -- set interface p2 ofport_request=2 type=dummy
ovs-ofctl mod-port br0 br0 noforward
NETFILENAME="qos_test"
ADDR1=1.1.0.1
ADDRD=1.1.0.2
get_netfile() {{
    cat <<EOF
<netprofile name="$NETFILENAME" type="qos" uuid="245324" version="1">
<flow direction="in" dl_type="ip" nw_dst="$ADDR1/24" nw_proto="icmp" priority="15">
<action burst="2" domain="interface" id="5" rate="1" unit="kbps" />
</flow>
</netprofile>
na-ctl --retry=-1 netprofile define --content "$(get_netfile)"
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 1
PRTNAME="6cecd756-a718-4ed6-9679-a19c38461d42_1"
get_portprofile() {{
    cat <<EOF
<portprofile name="$PRTNAME" type="compose">
  <netprofile name="$NETFILENAME" type="qos" />
</portprofile>
na-ctl portprofile define --content "$(get_portprofile)"
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 2
na-ctl add-listen --id=1 --event="set Interface p1 external_ids:port-profile=$PRTNAME"
ovs-vsctl set Interface p1 external_ids:port-profile=$PRTNAME
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 3
na-ctl wait --id=1  # so net-agentd knows the configuration
# Strips out uninteresting parts of flow output, as well as parts
# that vary from one run to another (e.g., timing and bond actions).
strip_timers () {{
    sed '
    s/duration:[0-9]*\.[0-9]*/duration:0.0/
    s/used:[0-9]*\.[0-9]*/used:0.0/
ovs-appctl time/warp 5000
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p1 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p1 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p1 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p1 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p1 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
# with burst size 2 (== 2000 bits). 4 packets, (240 bytes == 1920 bits) pass, but the last packet should hit the drop band.
(ovs-ofctl -O OpenFlow13 meter-stats br0 | strip_timers) > meterstats.txt
cat <<EOF > meterstats_expected.txt
OFPST_METER reply (OF1.3) (xid=0x2):
meter:2 flow_count:1 packet_in_count:5 byte_in_count:300 duration:0.0s bands:
0: packet_count:1 byte_count:60
diff --ignore-all-space meterstats_expected.txt meterstats.txt
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 4
test_dirTr
z0test icmp in direction qos: shell return code:%d
check_meter_testr
cmd_execute
format
	_test_dir
assertEqual
retr
test_icmp_in_direction_qos
Iz*TestQosFunction.test_icmp_in_direction_qosc
cd {test_dir}
ovs-vsctl add-br br0 -- set bridge br0 fail-mode=secure --     add-port br0 p1 -- set interface p1 ofport_request=1 type=dummy --     add-port br0 p2 -- set interface p2 ofport_request=2 type=dummy
ovs-ofctl mod-port br0 br0 noforward
NETFILENAME="qos_test"
ADDR1=1.1.0.1
ADDRD=1.1.0.2
# main difference with test_icmp_in_direction_qos is: direction => out 
get_netfile() {{
    cat <<EOF
<netprofile name="$NETFILENAME" type="qos" uuid="245324" version="1">
<flow direction="out" dl_type="ip" nw_dst="$ADDRD/24" nw_proto="icmp" priority="15">
<action burst="2" domain="interface" id="5" rate="1" unit="kbps" />
</flow>
</netprofile>
na-ctl --retry=-1 netprofile define --content "$(get_netfile)"
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 1
PRTNAME="6cecd756-a718-4ed6-9679-a19c38461d42_1"
get_portprofile() {{
    cat <<EOF
<portprofile name="$PRTNAME" type="compose">
  <netprofile name="$NETFILENAME" type="qos" />
</portprofile>
na-ctl portprofile define --content "$(get_portprofile)"
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 2
na-ctl add-listen --id=1 --event="set Interface p2 external_ids:port-profile=$PRTNAME"
ovs-vsctl set Interface p2 external_ids:port-profile=$PRTNAME
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 3
na-ctl wait --id=1  # so net-agentd knows the configuration
# Strips out uninteresting parts of flow output, as well as parts
# that vary from one run to another (e.g., timing and bond actions).
strip_timers() {{
    sed '
    s/duration:[0-9]*\.[0-9]*/duration:0.0/
    s/used:[0-9]*\.[0-9]*/used:0.0/
ovs-appctl time/warp 5000
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p1 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p1 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p1 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p1 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p1 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
# with burst size 2 (== 2000 bits). 4 packets, (240 bytes == 1920 bits) pass, but the last packet should hit the drop band.
(ovs-ofctl -O OpenFlow13 meter-stats br0 | strip_timers) > meterstats.txt
cat <<EOF > meterstats_expected.txt
OFPST_METER reply (OF1.3) (xid=0x2):
meter:2 flow_count:1 packet_in_count:5 byte_in_count:300 duration:0.0s bands:
0: packet_count:1 byte_count:60
diff --ignore-all-space meterstats_expected.txt meterstats.txt
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 4
1test icmp out direction qos: shell return code:%dr
test_icmp_out_direction_qos_
Kz+TestQosFunction.test_icmp_out_direction_qosc
cd {test_dir}
ADDR1=1.1.0.1
ADDRD=1.1.0.2
na-ctl add-listen --id=8 --event="remove Interface p2 external_ids:port-profile"
ovs-vsctl remove Interface p2 external_ids port-profile
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 33
na-ctl wait --id=8  # so net-agentd knows the configuration
ovs-appctl time/warp 5000
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p1 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p1 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p1 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p1 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p1 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
# sleep 0.5
recvin=$(ovs-vsctl get Interface p1 statistics:rx_packets)
sendout=$(ovs-vsctl get Interface p2 statistics:tx_packets)
if test $recvin -ne 5 || test $recvin -ne $recvout; then  # we expected there's no loss of packets during OVS translation
    exit 4
z4test icmp cancel direction qos: shell return code:%d)
test_icmp_cancel_qos
z$TestQosFunction.test_icmp_cancel_qosc
cd {test_dir}
ovs-vsctl add-br br0 -- set bridge br0 fail-mode=standalone --     add-port br0 p1 -- set interface p1 ofport_request=1 type=dummy --     add-port br0 p2 -- set interface p2 ofport_request=2 type=dummy --     add-port br0 p3 -- set interface p3 ofport_request=3 type=dummy
NETFILENAME="qos_test"
ADDR1=1.1.0.1
ADDR2=2.2.0.2
ADDR3=2.2.0.3
# mac learning
ovs-appctl ofproto/trace "in_port(1),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff)" -generate
ovs-appctl ofproto/trace "in_port(2),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0b,dst=ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff)" -generate
ovs-appctl ofproto/trace "in_port(3),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0c,dst=ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff)" -generate
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 1
ovs-appctl time/warp 1000
get_netfile() {{
    cat <<EOF
<netprofile name="$NETFILENAME" type="qos" uuid="245324" version="1">
<flow direction="out" dl_type="ip" nw_dst="$ADDR1/24" nw_proto="icmp" priority="15">
<action burst="2" domain="interface" id="5" rate="1" unit="kbps" />
</flow>
<flow direction="out" dl_type="ip" nw_dst="$ADDR2/24" nw_proto="icmp" priority="15">
<action burst="2" domain="interface" id="6" rate="1" unit="kbps" />
</flow>
</netprofile>
na-ctl --retry=-1 netprofile define --content "$(get_netfile)"
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 2
PRTNAME="6cecd756-a718-4ed6-9679-a19c38461d42_1"
get_portprofile() {{
    cat <<EOF
<portprofile name="$PRTNAME" type="compose">
  <netprofile name="$NETFILENAME" type="qos" />
</portprofile>
na-ctl portprofile define --content "$(get_portprofile)"
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 3
na-ctl add-listen --id=2 --event="set Interface p2 external_ids:port-profile=$PRTNAME"
ovs-vsctl set Interface p1 external_ids:port-profile=$PRTNAME
ovs-vsctl set Interface p2 external_ids:port-profile=$PRTNAME
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 4
na-ctl wait --id=2  # so net-agentd knows the configuration
ovs-appctl time/warp 1000
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p3 "in_port(3),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0c,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0a),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR3,dst=$ADDR1,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p3 "in_port(3),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0c,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0a),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR3,dst=$ADDR1,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p3 "in_port(3),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0c,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0a),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR3,dst=$ADDR1,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p3 "in_port(3),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0c,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0a),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR3,dst=$ADDR1,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p3 "in_port(3),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0c,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0a),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR3,dst=$ADDR1,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
# sleep 1  # need larger value
# with burst size 2 (== 2000 bits). 4 packets, (240 bytes == 1920 bits) pass, but the last packet should hit the drop band.
strip_timers() {{
    sed '
    s/duration:[0-9]*\.[0-9]*/duration:0.0/
    s/used:[0-9]*\.[0-9]*/used:0.0/
(ovs-ofctl -O OpenFlow13 meter-stats br0 | strip_timers) > meterstats.txt
pattern="flow_count:1 packet_in_count:5 byte_in_count:300 duration:0.0s bands:
0: packet_count:1 byte_count:60"
grep -Pazoq $pattern meterstats.txt
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 8
# sleep 2
ovs-appctl time/warp 1000
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p3 "in_port(3),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0c,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR3,dst=$ADDR2,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p3 "in_port(3),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0c,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR3,dst=$ADDR2,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p3 "in_port(3),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0c,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR3,dst=$ADDR2,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p3 "in_port(3),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0c,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR3,dst=$ADDR2,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p3 "in_port(3),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0c,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR3,dst=$ADDR2,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
# sleep 1
# with burst size 2 (== 2000 bits). 4 packets, (240 bytes == 1920 bits) pass, but the last packet should hit the drop band.
(ovs-ofctl -O OpenFlow13 meter-stats br0 | strip_timers) > meterstats2.txt
pattern="flow_count:1 packet_in_count:5 byte_in_count:300 duration:0.0s bands:
0: packet_count:1 byte_count:60"
grep -Pazoq $pattern meterstats2.txt
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 9
test_share_band_qos
hz#TestQosFunction.test_share_band_qos)	
__name__
__module__
__qualname__r

TestCaseBaser
mainr
<module>
